title: eriosema rufum var. rufum
intro: Eriosema rufum var. rufum, also known as the red pea bush or red eriosema, is a plant species in the family Fabaceae, native to tropical Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and seeds, which have been traditionally used to treat various health conditions. The leaves and seeds of the plant are reported to have antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antifungal properties, which may help to alleviate symptoms of fever, rheumatism, and respiratory infections. The plant's leaves and seeds can be prepared into herbal teas or infusions, as well as being used in traditional medicine preparations, such as ointments and decoctions.
